title: phlox pilosa subsp. latisepala
intro: Phlox pilosa subsp. latisepala, also known as wild phlox or fragrant phlox, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the phlox family (Polemoniaceae). The medicinal properties of Phlox pilosa subsp. latisepala include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties, and it is also believed to have health benefits in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The herbal preparations of Phlox pilosa subsp. latisepala include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
